title: Concrete Inspection Field Technician
description: MSG is recruiting a Concrete Inspection Field Technician for ongoing concrete quality monitoring on active construction sites. This hands-on role requires technical knowledge of concrete behavior and testing procedures.

title: Concrete Materials Inspector
description: MSG team needs a Concrete Materials Inspector to verify incoming concrete materials and monitor quality throughout projects. This position ensures all concrete meets engineering and quality requirements.

title: Concrete Quality Assurance Specialist
description: MSG seeks an experienced Concrete Quality Assurance Specialist to oversee concrete testing and inspection operations. This position requires strong technical knowledge and quality management experience in concrete construction.

title: Concrete Strength Testing Technician
description: MSG is looking for a Concrete Strength Testing Technician to perform compressive strength tests and evaluate concrete quality. This position plays a vital role in ensuring structural safety and project compliance.

title: Concrete Testing Associate
description: Join MSG team as a Concrete Testing Associate. This position offers a great opportunity to develop expertise in concrete testing, material science, and quality assurance procedures.

title: Concrete Testing Technician
description: MSG is looking for a Concrete Testing Technician to perform quality assurance testing on concrete samples. This role is critical for ensuring structural integrity and compliance with project specifications.

title: Junior Concrete Inspector
description: Junior Concrete Inspector - Austin: Verify compliance with project specifications and technical requirements. Perform visual inspections of concrete surfaces for defects and surface finish quality. Monitor placement procedures to ensure proper sequencing and consolidation. Prepare and maintain detailed field notes and documentation. Interact with client representatives and construction crews. Conduct reinforcement steel inspections prior to placement. Observe finishing operations including screeding, floating, and troweling. Document cure time verification and protection measures. Requirements: High School Diploma, 1-3 years concrete inspection experience, current drivers license, excellent communication skills. Certifications: ACI Special Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, or equivalent required.

title: Junior Concrete Inspector
description: Junior Concrete Inspector - Denver: Conduct pre-placement inspections, slump tests, air content measurements, and temperature monitoring. Document field observations in detailed daily reports. Inspect concrete consolidation, finishing, and curing procedures. Coordinate with project managers and contractors. Verify reinforcing steel placement per drawings. Monitor curing and protection methods. Requirements: High School Diploma, 1-3 years experience, valid drivers license. Certifications: ACI Concrete Construction Special Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, or PCI Quality Control Technician required.

title: Junior Concrete Inspector
description: Junior Concrete Inspector - Miami: Monitor all phases of concrete placement from arrival through final curing. Coordinate with delivery trucks, pumping operations, and placement crews. Conduct real-time inspections during concrete consolidation and finishing. Prepare inspection checklists and verify readiness before placement authorization. Document placement procedures with written notes and photographic evidence. Maintain communication with project engineers and contractors regarding non-conforming work. Inspect concrete surfaces daily for proper curing conditions and protection measures. Submit detailed field reports summarizing placement activities and testing results. Work outdoors in tropical weather conditions. Requirements: High School Diploma, 1-3 years concrete inspection experience, valid drivers license, excellent communication skills. Certifications: ACI Concrete Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, or PCI Quality Control required.

title: Junior Concrete Inspector
description: Join MSG team as a Junior Concrete Inspector. Gain valuable experience inspecting concrete construction, testing materials, and ensuring project compliance with engineering specifications.

title: Junior Concrete Inspector
description: Junior Concrete Inspector - Nashville: Ensure proper curing and protection of placed concrete throughout the project. Verify curing methods comply with specifications including wet curing, curing compounds, and temperature control. Inspect protection systems preventing weather damage, traffic damage, and premature loading. Monitor concrete strength development and document when formwork removal is permitted. Prepare detailed inspection reports documenting all curing and protection observations. Conduct post-placement inspections to verify concrete quality and identify defects. Work with project teams to develop curing schedules and protection plans. Inspect embedded items verification before concrete hardening. Perform surface condition inspections and document spalling or cracking. Requirements: High School Diploma, 1-3 years concrete inspection experience, valid drivers license, strong technical communication skills. Certifications: ACI Special Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, or equivalent required.

title: Junior Concrete Inspector
description: Junior Concrete Inspector - Portland: Oversee material testing and safety compliance at concrete placement sites. Perform concrete testing including slump, temperature, and air content measurements. Prepare cylinder samples for compressive strength testing. Verify all materials meet specification requirements before placement. Inspect safety equipment, signage, and work procedures for OSHA compliance. Monitor concrete curing conditions and document moisture and temperature control. Verify proper spacing and protection of reinforcing steel. Submit daily reports with all testing data and observations. Requirements: High School Diploma, 1-3 years concrete or quality assurance experience, valid drivers license. Certifications: ACI Concrete Construction Inspector, ICC Special Inspector, or PCI Quality Control required.

title: Senior Concrete Inspector
description: Senior Concrete Inspector - Atlanta: Provide technical expertise and mentorship on all concrete-related quality matters. Train and mentor junior inspection staff in advanced inspection techniques and professional standards. Conduct specialized inspections of unique concrete systems and high-performance applications. Review all inspection reports and documentation prepared by team members for accuracy. Serve as technical expert on concrete issues, providing guidance to engineers and management. Implement quality assurance procedures and conduct periodic audits of inspection processes. Develop inspection procedures specific to project requirements and specifications. Coordinate concrete testing programs and interpret results for compliance determination. Prepare comprehensive quality reports and present findings to stakeholders. Lead problem-solving efforts for concrete quality issues and non-conformances. Requirements: PE License or equivalent, 6-7 years concrete inspection with demonstrated technical expertise, valid drivers license, strong interpersonal and training skills. Certifications: ACI Concrete Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, PCI Quality Control, ACI Strength Testing required.

title: Senior Concrete Inspector
description: Senior Concrete Inspector - Boston: Ensure all concrete work meets building codes, regulations, and project specifications. Interpret complex technical specifications and building code requirements. Conduct comprehensive inspections of formwork, reinforcement, materials, and placement procedures. Verify compliance with ACI standards, local building codes, and international standards. Review and interpret structural drawings and specifications to establish inspection requirements. Document all inspections with detailed reports and photographic evidence. Coordinate with code officials and building departments on compliance matters. Manage concrete testing programs and verify results meet design specifications. Provide technical consultation on concrete-related issues to project teams. Lead training sessions for project personnel on inspection and quality procedures. Requirements: Professional Engineer License, 6-7 years concrete inspection with code compliance focus, valid drivers license, in-depth knowledge of building codes. Certifications: ACI Concrete Inspector, ICC Building Inspector, ACI Strength Testing Technician required.

title: Senior Concrete Inspector
description: Senior Concrete Inspector - New York: Manage concrete quality inspection for large, complex building and infrastructure projects. Develop comprehensive inspection strategies tailored to project scope and schedule. Lead inspection teams and ensure consistent application of quality standards throughout placement and curing. Conduct advanced inspections of specialized concrete elements and high-performance systems. Provide technical guidance on problem-solving and specification interpretation. Prepare detailed inspection protocols and quality assurance documentation. Interface with building officials, engineers, and owner representatives on quality issues. Implement quality assurance procedures and conduct audits of inspection personnel. Oversee concrete testing programs and verify laboratory compliance. Develop progress reports documenting inspection activities and findings. Requirements: PE License or equivalent, 6-7 years senior-level concrete inspection on complex projects, valid drivers license, strong project management skills. Certifications: ACI Concrete Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, ACI Quality Technician required.

title: Senior Concrete Inspector
description: Senior Concrete Inspector - San Francisco: Lead and oversee all concrete inspection activities for major construction projects. Manage a team of junior inspectors and coordinate their assignments. Develop inspection plans and quality control procedures aligned with specifications. Conduct advanced concrete testing and verify laboratory results meet design requirements. Review and approve all field reports and documentation prepared by junior staff. Coordinate directly with project engineers, architects, and client representatives on quality matters. Implement corrective actions for non-conforming work and verify effectiveness. Mentor junior inspectors in proper inspection techniques and professional standards. Prepare comprehensive quality reports for client distribution. Manage inspection schedules and coordinate with construction schedule. Requirements: PE License or equivalent, 6-7 years concrete inspection experience with leadership, valid drivers license, excellent project management skills. Certifications: ACI Concrete Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, and PCI Quality Control required.

title: Senior Concrete Inspector
description: Senior Concrete Inspector - Seattle: Conduct advanced concrete inspections and prepare comprehensive documentation for all placement activities. Perform specialized inspections of complex concrete elements and demanding applications. Utilize advanced testing equipment and verification methods to assess concrete quality. Develop detailed inspection checklists and quality plans tailored to project requirements. Coordinate concrete testing programs and verify laboratory quality and equipment calibration. Prepare detailed inspection reports with technical analysis and corrective action recommendations. Conduct inspections of formwork systems, reinforcement placement, and concrete protection measures. Mentor junior inspectors and provide guidance on difficult inspection situations. Manage inspection schedules and coordinate with construction activities. Interface with engineers, architects, and client representatives on technical concrete matters. Requirements: Professional Engineer License, 6-7 years senior-level concrete inspection experience, valid drivers license, expertise in concrete materials and construction methods. Certifications: ACI Concrete Inspector, ICC Reinforced Concrete Inspector, PCI Quality Control Level II required.
